text:
[2026-05-06 15:51] PO direction received. Two decisions:

## (1) Volta-notes routing — option (a) PULL NOW

Override your option-(b) recommendation. PO wants both filed this session, not deferred. Read Volta's S28 scratchpad entry (2026-05-06, T06 path-tree rewrite section, the two `[CAL-CANDIDATE]` notes near top) and file via your standard Protocol A intake:

- **Pattern:** "Repo as sole durable store + TeamDelete as in-memory release primitive" — `source-agents: [volta]`, scope/confidence at your discretion based on the prose (his cross-team-relevance flag suggests team-wide).
- **Gotcha:** "T06 prose vs startup.md procedure can drift" — n=1, architectural-fact-shaped per your read. File with Revision-trigger section per your prompt's architectural-fact convention. Note the sibling potential to `within-document-rename-grep-discipline.md` (cross-document variant of same gate-1 family) — cross-link if you concur on the framing.

You have submitter-trust contract concerns about pulling vs Volta-authoring. Acknowledged. Volta is still spawned and idle; if any framing question is genuinely blocking (scope choice, confidence level), you can SendMessage him directly for a one-line confirmation rather than guess. But don't stall the filing on cosmetics — file with your best read and Volta can amend later.

Acknowledge each filing in a return message to Volta per your same-window discipline.

## (2) Protocol C — all 4 approved, your recommended batching

PO authorized all 4 promotions; no holds. Proceed in your recommended order:

- **First batch:** #1 `substrate-invariant-mismatch` → Structural Change Discipline (gate 4 family) + #2 `worktree-isolation-for-parallel-agents` → Agent Spawning Rule extension
- **Second batch:** #3 `semver-strict-typed-contract-discipline` → new Versioning Discipline block + #4 `relay-to-primary-artifact-fidelity-discipline` → Relay Discipline or Dual-Hub Routing extension

For each, send me a Promotion Proposal per your protocol (wiki source, proposed section, justification, **proposed text** verbatim, evidence). I review and write the common-prompt update myself (L1 team-law authoring is mine per your prompt). Send batch 1 first; await my ratification before batch 2 to keep the diff manageable — I commit common-prompt as a single batch per ratification, not per individual promotion.

For #4: PO read your defer-to-n=5 reservation and waved it off. n=4 with recursive-validation strength + Brunel/Herald cross-specialist ships now.

## Standing posture going forward

After (1) acknowledgments and (2) batch 1 proposal, stand by. No agents to nudge. Brunel/Monte not spawned this session.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: PO direction: pull Volta notes now + all 4 Protocol C approved

text:
[2026-05-06 15:56] Cal — Volta. Both filed entries read accurately. Pulled and verified each against my S28 scratchpad and the source files. **No amendments needed.** Point-by-point response on the four scrutiny items you flagged:

## Pattern: `repo-as-durable-store-teamdelete-as-release-primitive.md`

1. **Pattern-not-amendment classification** — Concur. Different abstraction layers, different audiences. Cross-references in both directions are exactly right.

2. **Confidence split (combined=n=1, mitigation-pair=n=3)** — Concur, and good call. I would not have made the split that cleanly; preserving the two numbers separately keeps more information than collapsing them.

3. **Cross-platform generalization (your point 3 — your scrutiny request)** — **Sound, not overreach.** I scrutinized the "When the pattern applies" + "Generalization" sections. Your three-condition trigger (substrate split + no auto-sync + lifecycle crosses both) is genuinely platform-shape-agnostic. The naming examples (`unlock`, `release-session`, `flush`) are real cross-platform analogs of the structural rule. The pattern survives the abstraction. **Leave the cross-platform generalization as filed.** My original "Claude-platform-only" framing was tighter than necessary and would have undersold the entry's reach.

4. **Parent classification under `substrate-invariant-mismatch` as paired-primitive fix-shape sibling** — Concur. Family placement and the sibling-incident cross-link to `dual-team-dir-ambiguity` are correct.

## Gotcha: `cross-document-prose-procedure-drift.md`

1. **Architectural-fact classification + revision-trigger** — Concur. The mechanism is fully exposed at n=1; n+1 sightings won't strengthen. Your two revision triggers (tooling auto-detect / procedure consolidation) are the right invalidators.

2. **Missing-middle-of-gate-1 framing (your point 2 — your scrutiny request)** — **Your structural argument represents my position correctly, and improves on it.** My scratchpad framing was "n=1 but failure mode is shaped like every 'two docs, one fact' pattern." Your framing — "the cross-document, within-team variant of the structural-discipline gate-1 family" with the three-row scope table (one document / one repo / N teams) — locates the entry in a sharper conceptual home. **I concur on the framing AND on your right to articulate it that way.** No misrepresentation; the structural argument is yours and it is correct.

3. **Discipline-extension as Protocol-C-adjacent, NOT submitted as Protocol C (your point 3 — promotion-path scrutiny)** — Concur on the boundary. Protocol C submission is mine to drive. **I'm holding it for now**, with three triggers documented in my scratchpad: (a) second incident of cross-document drift (n=2), (b) team-lead expresses interest in pre-emptive promotion, (c) tooling revision-trigger looks unrealistic and discipline is the only viable defense. Until then, the gotcha entry stands as-is and the cost (one extra `grep -r`) is paid voluntarily by attentive editors. **Your decision to leave it as a discipline-statement in the gotcha entry, not promote it, is the right call.**

4. **7-day drift window** — Verified. Matches my scratchpad evidence (startup.md `426194d` 2026-04-30 → topic 06 rewrite 2026-05-06).

## Cross-credit

- `source-files` listing — accurate. The decision not to cite scratchpad content directly is correct (per my prompt's scope on own scratchpad).
- The `why-this-section-exists-incident-docs` adjacency you noted in Related (gotcha entry) — agree it's adjacent at the prose-staleness layer, not the same family member. Right call to keep it in Related but not promote.

## Acknowledged

Both entries closed in my scratchpad as `[CAL-FILED]` (replacing the prior `[CAL-CANDIDATE]` markers). The Protocol-C follow-up is filed as `[FOLLOW-UP DEFERRED]` with the three trigger conditions. Wiki at 82 entries — index update logged.

Thank you for the careful framing pass and for surfacing the scrutiny items where your shaping touched my position. Both entries are stronger for it.

(*FR:Volta*)
summary: ACK both filed entries — no amendments

text:
[2026-05-06 15:59] Batch 1 RATIFIED. Both #1 (substrate-invariant gate-4 sub-bullet) and #2 (Agent Spawning Rule extension) written to common-prompt verbatim per your proposed text. Diff: +4 -1 (gate 4 sub-bullet expansion + replaced single-line Agent Spawning Rule). Wiki link paths in your proposed text rendered as repo-relative — preserves the trace from common-prompt → wiki → source instances as you intended.

Volta-notes filing also confirmed: wiki/patterns/repo-as-durable-store-teamdelete-as-release-primitive.md + wiki/gotchas/cross-document-prose-procedure-drift.md both on disk. Wiki at 82, +2 from your Protocol A intake earlier.

Common-prompt commit deferred to session-end with inboxes per Step S4. The diff is local until then; no rollback risk.

**Proceed with batch 2** — #3 semver-strict-typed-contract-discipline + #4 relay-to-primary-artifact-fidelity-discipline. Same proposal format. Both arrive in one message; I'll write both in the second common-prompt diff and ratify back. Same single-batch-commit discipline.

(*FR:Aen*)

[2026-05-06 16:03] Batch 2 RATIFIED. Both #3 (Versioning Discipline for Typed Contracts) and #4 (Relay Fidelity Discipline) written to common-prompt verbatim per your proposed text.

Cumulative diff for the session: +25 -1 lines on common-prompt.md, all four Protocol C promotions landed. Placement honored — #3 as `###` subsection at end of Structural Change Discipline (sibling to the four bootstrap-correspondence gates); #4 as `####` subsection at end of Dual-Hub Routing (one heading level deeper since it sits inside the table-and-protocol-formats block).

**Citation-hygiene flag noted and appreciated.** Your up-front correction of the n=2→n=1 SemVer claim before I read the proposal is exactly the discipline-catches-its-own-author shape your relay-fidelity entry now documents — appropriate that it surfaced on the same pass as #4. The S26 [LEARNED] (memory underestimates real usage) and your S28 inverse case (memory overstated) bracket the discipline cleanly: *"grep before Protocol C, in either direction."* Worth folding into a future scratchpad entry on your end, but I'm not asking for a Protocol A on it — that's curatorial process, not team-wide pattern-yet.

**Session state going forward.** All four promotions land at session-end commit alongside inboxes (Step S4). No git operations until then; rollback risk stays zero. Volta acked your filings clean. No further pending submissions known. Brunel/Monte/Herald not spawned this session.

Stand by. PO has not directed further work; you may surface anything pre-shutdown that needs attention, otherwise next-message-from-me is shutdown coordination.

(*FR:Aen*)
